lessons.body: System.out.print writes text as-is, while System.out.println appends a line break that judges usually compare exactly.
lessons.title: Stdout

lessons.body: Local variables have declared types; primitives hold values directly and references point at objects such as String.
lessons.title: Variables and types

lessons.body: Integer division, remainder, casts, and numeric promotion decide the value before it ever reaches stdout.
lessons.title: Numbers and operators

lessons.body: String is immutable; methods such as trim, substring, charAt, and equals return or compare values without changing the original.
lessons.title: Strings

lessons.body: if selects a branch, loops repeat work, and each block must move the same typed values toward the final answer.
lessons.title: Control flow

lessons.body: A method signature declares parameter types and a return type; return sends the computed value back to the caller.
lessons.title: Methods

lessons.body: Coding-test Java usually reads System.in once, splits whitespace, then converts tokens before doing numeric work.
lessons.title: Input parsing

lessons.body: Arrays keep a fixed length, while List, Map, and Set cover growable order, keyed lookup, and uniqueness.
lessons.title: Arrays and collections

lessons.body: A class defines fields and behavior; new creates an object whose instance methods read or change that state.
lessons.title: Classes and objects

lessons.body: A constructor initializes each new object before methods run; overloaded constructors provide different entry points.
lessons.title: Constructors

lessons.body: private fields protect representation; public methods expose the operations callers are allowed to perform.
lessons.title: Encapsulation

lessons.body: static fields and methods belong to the class, not one object, so they are shared through the class name.
lessons.title: Static members

lessons.body: enum names a fixed set of constants, and switch expressions turn those constants into explicit result branches.
lessons.title: Enum and switch

lessons.body: try/catch handles recoverable failures; checked exceptions must be caught or declared in a method signature.
lessons.title: Exceptions

lessons.body: Generics let one class or method preserve the caller's element type instead of falling back to Object casts.
lessons.title: Generics

lessons.body: An interface names behavior a class promises to implement; callers can depend on that contract instead of the concrete class.
lessons.title: Interfaces

lessons.body: Inheritance reuses an is-a relationship; composition keeps a helper object as a field when behavior is only a has-a dependency.
lessons.title: Inheritance and composition

lessons.body: A record declares an immutable data carrier and gives you a constructor, accessors, equals, hashCode, and toString.
lessons.title: Records

lessons.body: Optional<T> makes a maybe-present value explicit and asks the caller to map, filter, or provide a fallback.
lessons.title: Optional

lessons.body: A lambda supplies behavior to a pipeline, and a stream processes elements only when a terminal operation consumes it.
lessons.title: Streams and lambdas

lessons.body: Comparator objects define ordering for sorted collections and list sorting without changing the stored type.
lessons.title: Comparators and sorting

lessons.body: try-with-resources closes AutoCloseable values automatically after the block, even when reading or parsing fails.
lessons.title: Try-with-resources

lessons.body: A package names a namespace across files; in this single-file judge, imports are the practical way to use packaged JDK classes.
lessons.title: Packages and imports

lessons.body: Annotations attach metadata to declarations; tools and frameworks can read them without changing normal method execution.
lessons.title: Annotations

lessons.body: sealed restricts which classes or records may implement a hierarchy, making closed domain alternatives visible to readers.
lessons.title: Sealed classes

lessons.body: Small testable methods let assertions check behavior before main prints; AssertionError is the simplest failure signal.
lessons.title: Testing and assert

lessons.body: Hash-based collections rely on equals and hashCode agreeing about which objects represent the same value.
lessons.title: equals and hashCode

lessons.body: Overloading chooses a method by argument types, while varargs gathers remaining arguments into an array parameter.
lessons.title: Overloading and varargs
